title: noCMS starter

items.title: Publish with one click
items.content_text: Publish with one click Editing, preview and theming happen at runtime in the browser — no build in the loop. Publishing is a discrete, asynchronous GitHub Action that prerenders the same component tree you previewed to static HTML. What you preview is exactly what you publish.
items.summary: Editing is instant in the browser; publishing is an async GitHub Action.

items.title: Theming with tokens, not code
items.content_text: Theming with tokens, not code Colors, fonts, spacing and radius live in a flat file. They compile to CSS variables in the browser, so changing a token restyles the whole site instantly. Runtime theming never triggers a build — that is an architecture invariant, not an optimization.
items.summary: Design tokens compile to CSS variables at runtime — restyle with no rebuild.

items.title: Hello from a repo-backed CMS
items.content_text: Hello from a repo backed CMS This post lives at . The ② batch tier reads the whole collection, emits , and the homepage's island lists it — no server, no database, just files the site serves. Edit this file on GitHub, publish, and the feed regenerates on the next Action run.
items.summary: Why the repo is the database, and what that buys you.

description: Notes from a site that lives in its own git repo.
